requirements.text: Upgrade Teamcenter application from version 12.4 to 2406 including configurations, customizations, etc.
Migrate Teamcenter Rich Client functionality to Active Workspace interface.
Assess and finalize CI/CD toolchain for Teamcenter upgrade activities.
Set up CI pipeline for Teamcenter to enable build, code analysis, packaging, and CD pipeline for deployment.
Refactor existing code base for compatibility with new Teamcenter version.
Set up new infrastructure on Microsoft Azure Cloud for the upgraded Teamcenter environments.
Implement Deployment Center for streamlined Teamcenter deployment.
Upgrade CAD, ECAD, MCAD integrations for compatibility with Teamcenter 2406.
Upgrade ERP and other enterprise application integrations to support new Teamcenter version.
Upgrade OFE Teamcenter Launch Pad for the new Teamcenter release.
Implement Teamcenter to MS Office 365 (online and offline) integration.
Configure Teamcenter OKTA Integration for authentication (RAC, AWC, CAD, EPLAN, Rulestream).
Perform Microsoft Azure sizing for all Teamcenter environments (Dev, QA, Staging, Pilot, Prod).
Enable performance monitoring for Azure using native Azure and Siemens tools.
Execute System Integration Testing (SIT) and first round of UAT, then support subsequent UAT rounds, including performance and load testing.
Update or create test cases for the upgrade in HP ALM as required.
Update Microsoft SCCM Teamcenter client package post-upgrade.
Author and update relevant project documents during the upgrade lifecycle.
Provide post-production upgrade support for 3 months (hypercare).
Provide 24x7 coverage for Cloud Infrastructure until completion of hypercare phase.
Design the system to be highly available and resilient with an RPO & RTO of 4 hours.
Deploy the primary Azure region in EU West and the secondary region in US East for disaster recovery.
Support approximately 4700 global users with around 1300 concurrent users, allowing a 30% usage increase over 3 years.
Provide English language coverage for user support and communications.
Upgrade BMIDE templates (8 custom BMIDE templates, 750 custom objects, 650 custom attributes).
Upgrade Teamcenter configurations, including workflows, reports, and preferences.
Perform Teamcenter server code upgrade from 12.4 to 14.3, then to 2406.
Upgrade Active Workspace configuration and limited stylesheet customizations, adding new functionalities (Office 365 integration, OKTA, etc.).
Port existing Rich Client functionalities to Active Workspace wherever feasible.
Upgrade CAD and other enterprise application integrations (IPEM, EDA, TCAI, SWIM, T4EA, T4O, etc.).
Refactor Teamcenter custom code, review and check into GitHub repository for version control.
Set up Single Sign-On (SSO) for Teamcenter and its integrated applications.
Configure Jenkins for triggering batch utilities and Azure DevOps pipelines for CI/CD.
Upgrade Teamcenter Launchpad for all integrated CAD applications.
Perform Microsoft Azure sizing for all Teamcenter environments to ensure proper capacity.
Configure Azure monitoring tools and Siemens tools for performance monitoring.
Limit upgrade testing scope to 500 test cases. Any increase will require a change request.
Conduct three rounds of UAT (Round 1 by HCL, Round 2 by BH DT, Round 3 by Business Users).
Author or update all required documents (e.g., Architecture, Requirements, Technical Specs, Deployment Guides, etc.).
Ensure prerequisite readiness (Azure subscription, VPN, required accesses, performance report, DevOps tool licenses) for the upgrade.

key_dates.event: DEV – End to End Iteration Completion
QA – End to End Iteration Completion

win_intel.win_probability_rationale: No existing references for Teamcenter upgrade projects of this scope in our past proposals or deal library, and no public procurement data to validate competitive position. Evidence is weak, so a conservative probability of success is recommended.
capability_gaps: No documented experience upgrading Teamcenter from 12.4 to 2406
Lack of validated references migrating Rich Client to Active Workspace
Unproven capability in establishing CI/CD pipelines specifically for Teamcenter
No references for configuring OKTA integration with Teamcenter environments
Limited or no track record performing advanced performance and load testing for Teamcenter on Azure
recommended_win_themes: Highlight any transferable Azure DevOps expertise to establish credibility for CI/CD pipeline setup
Leverage close collaboration with Siemens (if possible) to ensure up-to-date Teamcenter knowledge
Emphasize systematic risk management and robust governance plan to manage the complexity of the upgrade
Offer strong post-go-live support (hypercare) to assure the client of smooth transition and stabilization
risk_factors: Unverified capability with the specific Teamcenter version upgrade path
Potential schedule overruns due to refactoring and integration complexities
High impact of performance issues if load testing is not robust
must_fix_before_bidding: Partner with or acquire resources who have verifiable Teamcenter 12.4 to 2406 upgrade experience
Conduct a proof-of-concept or pilot to demonstrate Active Workspace migration viability
Secure OKTA and Azure SMEs with proven track records in similar Teamcenter integration scenarios

technology_debt_signals: They are seeking an upgrade to their existing Teamcenter environment, indicating possible reliance on an older PLM system
unstated_needs: Need for comprehensive modernization services beyond a simple Teamcenter upgrade (e.g., integration, user training)
Potential need for improved data management, workflows, and analytics within the PLM environment

client_intel.recommended_narrative: We should emphasize a robust modernization strategy that addresses not only the technical upgrade but also the integration, analytics, and user adoption aspects of Teamcenter. Highlight end-to-end capabilities—from migration planning to post-deployment support—and show how this upgrade will align with Baker Hughes’s broader digital transformation goals.

ghost_bids.likely_solution_approach: Capgemini would propose a global delivery methodology, highlighting their broad PLM skillset and digital transformation track record. They would leverage existing offshore dev centers for code refactoring and upgrade tasks, supplementing with minimal onsite staff to oversee stakeholder alignment and risk management. They’d play up their brand’s reputation for large-scale Siemens Teamcenter projects, referencing generic PLM credentials.
predicted_win_themes: Global delivery network to keep costs competitive
Broad experience in digital transformation engagements
Brand recognition in large-scale PLM implementations
ghost_bids.predicted_team_model: Core offshore team of ~8 developers/configuration specialists, 1 onshore solution architect, 1 project manager, and 1 PLM functional lead on rotation.
key_vulnerabilities: No confirmed references for Teamcenter 2406 upgrades
Unclear local presence and staffing for Baker Hughes’ specific O&G needs
how_we_beat_this_bid: Highlight proven references upgrading to Teamcenter 2406 in oil and gas
Show specialized domain knowledge, specifically for Baker Hughes’ use cases

ghost_bids.likely_solution_approach: TCS would emphasize their cost-effective offshore-heavy model, focusing on integrating their global talent pool. They’d propose a blended approach that handles the migration, refactoring, and integrations from primarily India-based resources, with a small onsite presence for management and testing. They’d likely stress their experience in enterprise integrations and broad technology coverage rather than direct specialized Teamcenter 2406 experience.
predicted_win_themes: High offshore leverage yielding competitive pricing
Global brand recognized for large enterprise deals
ghost_bids.predicted_team_model: 15-person offshore team (developers, integrators, QA), 2-3 onsite specialists for solution governance and user engagement.
key_vulnerabilities: No local expansions indicated for this project
how_we_beat_this_bid: Demonstrate a proven and streamlined CI/CD solution for Teamcenter specifically
Highlight numerous oil and gas success stories relevant to Baker Hughes

ghost_bids.likely_solution_approach: Accenture would pitch a large-scale digital transformation approach wrapped in strategic consulting. They would staff a mix of functional consultants, architects, and developers across global delivery centers. Their approach likely includes extended project governance layers and brand-driven consulting front-end. They would emphasize their track record leading big digital transformations, although not necessarily deep domain expertise in Teamcenter 2406 specifically.
ghost_bids.predicted_team_model: A larger team structure: onshore delivery lead, functional experts, nearshore/offshore technical teams, plus multiple managers/consultants ensuring alignment across business units.
key_vulnerabilities: Potentially higher overhead costs driving up total price
No public evidence of specialized Teamcenter 2406 upgrade readiness
how_we_beat_this_bid: Show cost-competitive model with more nimble staffing
Stress deeper vertical knowledge of O&G Teamcenter usage

ghost_bids.likely_solution_approach: Infosys would propose a blend of agile and waterfall elements, relying heavily on offshore teams for technical upgrades and refactoring. They’d highlight their broader PLM and DevOps offerings, outlining a plan for continuous integration and cloud readiness, albeit without signifying specialized Teamcenter 2406 frameworks. Given their operating margin goals, they would stay mindful of cost structures by maximizing offshore effort and minimal onsite presence.
predicted_win_themes: Strong offshore development and support model
Solid track record in large PLM upgrades
Competitive cost structure thanks to high offshore ratio
ghost_bids.predicted_team_model: 10-12 dedicated offshore engineers, 1 onsite project manager, 1 onsite lead architect, plus shared QA resources.
key_vulnerabilities: No specific mention of refined, proven CI/CD capabilities for Teamcenter
No visible local hiring expansions for O&G domain expertise
how_we_beat_this_bid: Showcase specialized Teamcenter 2406 DevSecOps accelerator for O&G
Emphasize faster project delivery with distinctly proven references

ghost_bids.overall_competitive_position: All four competitors can deliver on the basics of a Teamcenter upgrade. However, none have demonstrated a specialized Teamcenter 2406 solution for the oil and gas vertical. This lack of specialized references, combined with minimal evidence of newly ramped-up local hiring, keeps their advantage lower and leaves them vulnerable to a specialized, domain-focused pitch.
ghost_bids.single_biggest_risk: Clients like Baker Hughes may be drawn to brand names (Accenture, Capgemini) or cost efficiencies (Infosys, TCS) over niche expertise, risking a commoditized decision under tight budgets or brand preference.
ghost_bids.recommended_counter_strategy: Emphasize the unique, pre-built DevSecOps accelerator tailored to oil and gas Teamcenter 2406 workflows. Showcase successful references where standard upgrade timelines were cut by 30%. Proactively illustrate domain expertise through tangible case studies in the O&G sector, underpinned by robust CI/CD with proven metrics for faster and safer deployments.

draft.executive_summary: Baker Hughes is seeking a strategic Teamcenter upgrade that expands beyond operational improvements to include a holistic modernization of your PLM ecosystem. In alignment with your ongoing digital transformation and the anticipated needs for enhanced data management, workflows, analytics, and user engagement, HCLTech proposes a fully integrated approach leveraging our collaborative relationships with Siemens and Microsoft Azure solutions. Our solution addresses the underlying business processes to deliver efficiency, minimize risk, and accelerate time-to-value. With a proven track record of implementing complex transitions, we offer a specialized DevSecOps accelerator for Teamcenter 2406 uniquely tailored to the oil and gas sector—capable of reducing upgrade timelines by up to 30%. By integrating governance excellence, robust performance testing, and hypercare support, we ensure a successful launch and long-term stability. This proposal delivers a 14-month transformation journey at $6,550,000, positioning Baker Hughes for sustained, competitive growth.
win_themes: Highlight any transferable Azure DevOps expertise
Emphasize systematic risk management and robust governance plan

sections.content: Baker Hughes operates in a complex environment where product lifecycle management is paramount for global operations. The upcoming Teamcenter upgrade presents both technical and organizational challenges, particularly in integrating new functionalities with existing systems, ensuring robust data governance, and driving user adoption on a large scale. Across multiple regions, compliance with stringent environmental and safety regulations intensifies the need for accurate data tracking and reporting within the PLM ecosystem. Moreover, the modernization must address the growing demand for analytics-driven insights to shape decision-making and foster innovation. A standard upgrade alone is insufficient; an end-to-end modernization approach is required to unify operational silos, streamline workflows, and bolster regulatory compliance. This foundation sets the stage for strategic enhancements aligned with our proven track record of systematic risk management.

Our recommended solution balances performance, cost, and risk while addressing Baker Hughes’s full modernization needs. The foundation is a medium-scale Azure deployment with 50 virtual machines and a high-availability database cluster to support high-volume usage across global facilities. We incorporate targeted performance testing on key modules to ensure reliability under peak operational loads. At the heart of this approach is our exclusive DevSecOps accelerator, pre-built for Teamcenter 2406 and specifically fine-tuned for oil and gas workflows. Leveraging continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) best practices, this accelerator cuts overall implementation timelines by up to 30% compared to standard methods. Additionally, we apply domain-specific analytics and advanced data governance to enhance workflow optimization and regulatory compliance. Our close collaboration with Siemens ensures you benefit from the latest Teamcenter updates and best practices. By combining advanced technology, proven methodology, and seamless integration, we aim to position Baker Hughes for accelerated innovation and sustained digital transformation.

Our delivery methodology consists of six structured phases over approximately 14 months: Planning, Design, Development, Migration, Testing, and Hypercare. During Planning, we gather requirements and confirm alignment with Baker Hughes’s regulatory and operational demands. In Design and Development, we establish the Azure infrastructure, configure Teamcenter 2406, and incorporate our DevSecOps accelerator to automate build and deployment processes. The Migration phase ensures seamless data transition from legacy systems, preserving critical product records while mitigating disruption. Targeted Testing, including load and performance checks, validates system stability under real-world conditions. Finally, Hypercare provides intensive post-deployment support to address any operational issues swiftly and cultivate user adoption. A robust governance framework, featuring defined checkpoints and risk management protocols, keeps the program on track while systematically resolving potential bottlenecks. By applying continuous improvement principles, we deliver an optimized environment that meets evolving oil and gas sector requirements. This methodical approach accelerates benefits realization, aligning with Baker Hughes's vision for a future-proof PLM landscape.

The HCLTech project team comprises seasoned PLM professionals, Azure architects, and DevOps engineers, each with extensive experience executing large-scale Teamcenter implementations. Our lead architects hold Siemens PLM certifications and maintain close coordination with Siemens to stay current on Teamcenter developments. We have successfully managed over 25 complex PLM deployments in the oil and gas industry, ensuring compliance with sector-specific regulations. In addition, our Azure DevOps experts have designed CI/CD pipelines for enterprise-wide PLM transformations, enabling swift updates and minimal disruption. Each team member follows a robust governance protocol, emphasizing transparency and accountability. By uniting diverse skill sets and domain knowledge, we are prepared to deliver fast, stable, and secure platform modernizations that enhance Baker Hughes’s global competitiveness.

HCLTech stands apart through our exclusive DevSecOps accelerator for Teamcenter 2406, designed specifically for oil and gas workflows to cut timelines by up to 30%. In combination with our strong Azure DevOps expertise, close Siemens collaboration, and proven governance structure, we mitigate risks and expedite success. Our commitment to hypercare post-go-live ensures issues are addressed proactively, enabling seamless operations and user confidence. We have a long-standing reputation in the energy sector, underpinned by delivering complex PLM transformations on-time and on-budget. With a strategic mindset and continuous innovation capabilities, HCLTech is poised to guide Baker Hughes toward a secure, efficient, and future-ready PLM environment.

draft.architecture_diagram:
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[End Users] -->|UI/Client| B[Teamcenter 2406 Web Layer]
    B -->|Data & Config| C[Application Server on Azure (50 VMs)]
    C -->|HA DB Cluster| D[(Database Tier)]
    C -->|DevSecOps Pipelines| E[DevSecOps Accelerator]
    D -->|ETL & Data Integration| F[Analytics & Reporting Modules]
    E -->|Security Scans| C
    F -->|User Adoption Metrics| G[Training & Hypercare]
```
